key_points: Tested 7 AI image generators with identical prompts
Manus AI, Gemini, ChatGPT most realistic outputs
Flux handles complex multi-element prompts with highest fidelity
DALL-E 3 best for high-volume content creation with text
Flux better default for commercial teams
SD 3.5 better for self-hosting and local customization

stats.fact: AI image generator market valued at ~$484M in 2026, expected to reach $1.75B by 2034
Market growing at 32.5% CAGR, projected to reach $30B by 2033 by some estimates
65% of graphic designers use AI tools daily
42% of U.S. adults have tested AI image generators
78% of marketers planning to adopt AI image generation
Midjourney has ~19.83M users as of Jan 2026, with 1.2-2.5M daily active users
Flux.2 [max] and GPT Image 1.5 share top LM Arena leaderboard (Elo 1265 and 1264)
North America holds 40.34% of AI image generator market share
FLUX 1.1 Pro generates images in 4.5 seconds — fastest among quality-focused models
Stable Diffusion 3.5 Medium needs 9.9GB VRAM; Flux klein needs ~13GB VRAM for local use

tools.name: Midjourney v7
tools.best_for: Artistic quality, cinematic lighting, editorial photography, fantasy/sci-fi

tools.name: Flux Pro / Flux.2
tools.best_for: Photorealism, product shots, text rendering, complex multi-element prompts
pros: Handles complex prompts with highest fidelity
cons: Open weights limited to non-commercial for dev version
tools.pricing: API-based pricing; Flux schnell free (Apache 2.0), Flux dev free non-commercial

tools.name: GPT Image 1.5 / DALL-E
tools.best_for: Complex prompt comprehension, text in images, high-volume content, ChatGPT integration
cons: Requires ChatGPT Plus subscription for best access
tools.pricing: Free via Bing/Copilot (limited), ChatGPT Plus $20/mo, API pricing per image

tools.name: Ideogram v2
tools.best_for: Text rendering in images, logos, typography-heavy designs

tools.name: Adobe Firefly 3
tools.best_for: Commercial use with IP safety, Creative Cloud integration, enterprise
cons: Less capable than Midjourney/Flux for raw quality
tools.pricing: Standard $9.99/mo (2K credits), Pro $19.99/mo (4K credits), Premium $199.99/mo (50K credits)

tools.name: Leonardo.ai
tools.best_for: Game assets, character design, versatile creative work, budget-conscious users
pros: Strong for game and character art
cons: Can feel generic on default settings

tools.name: Stable Diffusion 3.5
tools.best_for: Local generation, customization, ControlNet workflows, budget-conscious
pros: Rich ecosystem of ControlNets and fine-tunes
Free for commercial use under $1M revenue
tools.pricing: Free (open source), enterprise license for >$1M revenue

tools.name: Google Imagen 3
tools.best_for: Fast generation, Google ecosystem integration, scale
tools.pricing: Via Google Cloud / Vertex AI pricing

angles: No single best tool: Midjourney for art, Flux for photorealism, DALL-E for comprehension, Ideogram for text — smart studios use 2-4
The LM Arena leaderboard: Flux.2 and GPT Image 1.5 are statistically tied at the top
Open source is viable: Stable Diffusion 3.5 and Flux schnell run on consumer hardware for zero marginal cost
Text rendering is the new battleground: Flux and Ideogram lead, Midjourney lags
Commercial safety matters: Adobe Firefly is the only tool with IP indemnification for enterprise
Speed gap closing: Flux at 4.5s vs Midjourney at 15-30s is a 3-6x difference that matters for production pipelines
keywords: AI image generator for commercial use
